div.p: William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have given and granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there, in exchange for the alms that they lost through the garrison, 4 merks from my mill of Cramond, and 33 shillings from the mill of Liberton, and from the ferm of Merchiston 20 shillings, and from the land of the furbisher 8 shillings, and from the ferm of Clermiston 5 shillings and 8 pence. For them to hold in free and quiet alms as freely and quietly as they hold and possess their other alms freely and quietly, until I shall be able to warrant to them the said alms which they lost through the garrison of Edinburgh, or until I shall have assigned an income of 9 merks elsewhere. As witnesses, Hugh bishop of St Andrews, Walter Oliphant the justice, Hugh Gifford, Walter of Berkeley, William de la Haye, Robert of Berkeley. At Haddington.
